titleStmt.title: Letter from Denis Mullally to the Under Secretary of Ireland, 2 August 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Denis Mullally

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Denis Mullally (1878-1919), Clerk of Bray Urban District Council, to the under secretary of Ireland concerning the retirement of the Bray Harbour Master, Mr. Scraggs, and his entitlement to a pension.At the time this letter was written Sir Robert Chalmers (1858-1938) was serving as under secretary of Ireland. Chalmers was sent to Ireland as replacement under secretary for Sir Matthew Nathan, who had resigned along with Augustine Birrell, his chief secretary, following the outbreak of the Easter Rising in 1916.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/5620/13283

correspAction.placeName: Bray Urban District Council, Town Hall, Bray, Co. Wicklow, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Bray Urban District Council Town Hall BRAY 2nd Ju Aug, 1916 Sir, I am directed by Bray Urban District Council to acknowledge
receipt of your letter of 28th Ult., and to state, in reply,
that they are desirous of granting Mr Scraggs a retiring
allowance on the ground of ill health under the provisions of
Section 1 of the Local Officers Superannuations Act (Ireland) 1869. A Medical Certificate testifying that Mr. Scraggs is unfit
to perform his duties is enclosed herewith. I am,
Sir,
Your obedient servant D. Mullally Clerk to the Council. The Under Secretary, Dublin Castle, Dublin. Ack. & in private T.G.'s
are sent to a retiring allowance
of 13/4 a week with
effect for the date of
Mr Scragg's retirement
I.J.T.
3.8.16
5 Prince of Wales Terrace, Bray. 1st August 1916 This is to certify that Thomas Scragg is
suffering from weak action of the heart, & debility,
and is unfit to perform the light work he has
hither to done. C Braw M.D.
